title.original: Lily Allen Drops Most Arena Dates on U.S. Tour in Favor of Theater and Amphitheater Shows
title.en: Lily Allen Drops Most Arena Dates on U.S. Tour in Favor of Theater and Amphitheater Shows
title.zh: 莉莉·艾伦取消美国大型场馆大多数演出，转向剧院和露天剧场
title.es: Lily Allen cancela la mayoría de fechas en arenas de su gira en EE.UU. en favor de teatros y anfiteatros

summary.original: Variety reports that British singer Lily Allen has canceled most of her arena shows on the U.S. leg of her “West End Girl” tour. The tour, which was highly acclaimed when performed earlier this year in U.S. theaters as a one-woman theatrical show, had raised questions about how it would translate to larger venues. Consequently, Allen and Live Nation chose to cancel most arena dates in North America and instead book new concerts in theaters and amphitheaters. The decision reflects a preference for more modestly sized venues rather than large arenas for these performances.
summary.en: Variety reports that British singer Lily Allen has canceled most of her arena shows on the U.S. leg of her “West End Girl” tour. The tour, which was highly acclaimed when performed earlier this year in U.S. theaters as a one-woman theatrical show, had raised questions about how it would translate to larger venues. Consequently, Allen and Live Nation chose to cancel most arena dates in North America and instead book new concerts in theaters and amphitheaters. The decision reflects a preference for more modestly sized venues rather than large arenas for these performances.
summary.zh: 据《综艺》报道，英国歌手莉莉·艾伦取消了其“West End Girl”美国巡演的大部分大型场馆演出。该巡演在美国剧场作为独奏戏剧形式获高度赞誉，演出转到大型场馆存在疑问。艾伦与Live Nation决定取消美国大多数大型场馆演出，改为预订剧院和露天剧场演出，偏好更小规模场地。
summary.es: Variety informa que la cantante británica Lily Allen canceló la mayoría de sus shows en arenas durante la gira estadounidense “West End Girl”. La gira, que fue muy aclamada en teatros como espectáculo unipersonal, planteaba dudas sobre su adaptación a grandes recintos. Allen y Live Nation optaron por cancelar la mayoría de fechas en arenas en América del Norte y reservar conciertos en teatros y anfiteatros, prefiriendo recintos más pequeños.

summary.es: El New York Times informa que EE.UU. e Irán han vuelto a acercarse a un conflicto abierto sobre el Estrecho de Hormuz. EE.UU. planea reinstaurar un bloqueo a puertos iraníes a partir del martes. Irán declaró haber atacado dos petroleros y disparado contra bases militares estadounidenses en la zona. The sequel will feature Superman, played by David Corenswet, teaming up with Lex Luthor to face the antagonist Brainiac. The film is scheduled for theatrical release on July 9, 2027. These details come directly from the article and related statements.

The sequel will feature Superman, played by David Corenswet, teaming up with Lex Luthor to face the antagonist Brainiac. The film is scheduled for theatrical release on July 9, 2027. These details come directly from the article and related statements.
summary.zh: 据《综艺》报道，演员Xolo Maridueña将在由詹姆斯·古恩执导的《超人》续集《明日之子》中继续饰演蓝甲虫（Jaime Reyes）一角。马里度尼亚首次演绎此角是在2023年由安赫尔·曼努埃尔·索托导演的影片中。续集中，戴维·科伦斯韦特饰演的超人与莱克斯·卢瑟联手对抗反派脑王。该片定于2027年7月9日上映。以上信息来自相关报道和声明。
summary.es: Variety informa que el actor Xolo Maridueña retomará su papel como Jaime Reyes, el Blue Beetle, en la próxima secuela de “Superman” titulada “Man of Tomorrow”, actualmente en producción y dirigida por James Gunn. Maridueña interpretó por primera vez a Blue Beetle en la película de 2023 dirigida por Ángel Manuel Soto. En la secuela, Superman, protagonizado por David Corenswet, se une a Lex Luthor para enfrentar al antagonista Brainiac. El estreno está programado para el 9 de julio de 2027. Estos detalles provienen directamente del artículo y declaraciones relacionadas.

title.original: How Strong Is the Democratic State AGs’ Lawsuit Aiming to Block Paramount’s Warner Bros. Takeover?
title.en: How Strong Is the Democratic State AGs’ Lawsuit Aiming to Block Paramount’s Warner Bros. Takeover?
title.zh: 民主党州总检察长阻止派拉蒙收购华纳兄弟的诉讼有多强？
title.es: ¿Qué tan sólida es la demanda de fiscales generales demócratas para bloquear la toma de Warner Bros. por Paramount?

summary.original: Variety covers a lawsuit filed by 12 Democratic state attorneys general seeking to block a merger between Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery. The states argue the merger violates antitrust laws by consolidating power in theatrical distribution, tentpole movies, and basic cable TV markets. Paramount disputes the claims and intends to vigorously fight the lawsuit. The states also filed for a temporary restraining order to pause the merger, with a court hearing scheduled for July 17. Separately, the Writers Guild of America has sued the merger, alleging it would reduce writers' pay and job opportunities. This summary is based on the information provided by Variety in their report.
summary.en: Variety covers a lawsuit filed by 12 Democratic state attorneys general seeking to block a merger between Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery. The states argue the merger violates antitrust laws by consolidating power in theatrical distribution, tentpole movies, and basic cable TV markets. Paramount disputes the claims and intends to vigorously fight the lawsuit. The states also filed for a temporary restraining order to pause the merger, with a court hearing scheduled for July 17. Separately, the Writers Guild of America has sued the merger, alleging it would reduce writers' pay and job opportunities. This summary is based on the information provided by Variety in their report.
summary.zh: 《综艺》报道，12位民主党州总检察长提起诉讼，试图阻止派拉蒙Skydance与华纳兄弟探索的合并，理由是该合并违反反垄断法规，集中掌控影院发行、大制作电影和基础有线电视市场。派拉蒙否认指控，将积极应诉。诉讼中州检察长还请求临时限制令暂停合并，法庭听证会定于7月17日。美国编剧工会也起诉此次合并，称会减少编剧薪酬和就业机会。以上内容基于《综艺》报道。
summary.es: Variety cubre una demanda presentada por 12 fiscales generales estatales demócratas que buscan bloquear la fusión entre Paramount Skydance y Warner Bros. Discovery. Los estados argumentan que la fusión viola las leyes antimonopolio al consolidar poder en la distribución teatral, películas de gran presupuesto y mercados de televisión básica por cable. Paramount impugna las acusaciones e intentará combatir vigorosamente la demanda. Los estados también solicitaron una orden de restricción temporal para detener la fusión, con una audiencia judicial prevista para el 17 de julio. Separadamente, el Gremio de Escritores de América ha demandado la fusión, alegando que reduciría el pago y las oportunidades laborales para guionistas. Este resumen se basa en la información proporcionada por Variety.

title.original: Trump says US to abandon proposed Strait of Hormuz cargo fee
title.en: Trump says US to abandon proposed Strait of Hormuz cargo fee
title.zh: 特朗普称美国放弃霍尔木兹海峡货运费用提议
title.es: Trump dice que EE.UU. abandonará la propuesta de tarifa del 20% para carga en el Estrecho de Hormuz

summary.original: According to the Financial Times, US President Donald Trump announced that the United States would abandon its proposed 20% fee on cargo shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Instead, Trump said the toll would be replaced by "massive" trade and investment deals with Gulf states. This change came just hours before the US resumed its blockade of Iranian ports amid renewed strikes between the US and Iran. The US Central Command stated that additional strikes were launched to degrade Iranian capabilities used to attack commercial shipping in the strait, a key waterway for global oil shipments. Iran responded with blasts in multiple cities and targeted US military facilities, highlighting the ongoing tension over control of the strait, which is strategically important. Trump had declared that the US was now the "guardian" of the Strait of Hormuz but dropped the fee plan after receiving numerous calls from Gulf leaders. The blockade and strikes coincide with significant impacts on oil prices and shipping traffic through the strait, which has slowed dramatically. This summary is based solely on the information supplied by the Financial Times.
summary.en: According to the Financial Times, US President Donald Trump announced that the United States would abandon its proposed 20% fee on cargo shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Instead, Trump said the toll would be replaced by "massive" trade and investment deals with Gulf states. This change came just hours before the US resumed its blockade of Iranian ports amid renewed strikes between the US and Iran. The US Central Command stated that additional strikes were launched to degrade Iranian capabilities used to attack commercial shipping in the strait, a key waterway for global oil shipments. Iran responded with blasts in multiple cities and targeted US military facilities, highlighting the ongoing tension over control of the strait, which is strategically important. Trump had declared that the US was now the "guardian" of the Strait of Hormuz but dropped the fee plan after receiving numerous calls from Gulf leaders. The blockade and strikes coincide with significant impacts on oil prices and shipping traffic through the strait, which has slowed dramatically. This summary is based solely on the information supplied by the Financial Times.
summary.zh: 据《金融时报》报道，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普宣布，美国将放弃对通过霍尔木兹海峡的货运收取20%费用的提议，改为与海湾国家签订“庞大”的贸易和投资协议。此举发生在美国因美伊冲突升级重新封锁伊朗港口数小时前。美军中央司令部称对伊朗发动更多打击，以削弱其攻击商业船只的能力。伊朗则在多城实施爆炸袭击并针对美军事设施，彰显双方围绕该重要石油航道的紧张局势。特朗普此前宣布美为海峡“守护者”，但在多位海湾领导人劝阻下放弃收费计划。该封锁和打击对油价和航运产生重大影响，航运流量明显减缓。以上信息基于《金融时报》报道。
summary.es: Según el Financial Times, el presidente estadounidense Donald Trump anunció que Estados Unidos abandonaría su propuesta de imponer una tarifa del 20% sobre la carga que atraviesa el Estrecho de Hormuz. En cambio, Trump indicó que la tarifa sería reemplazada por acuerdos comerciales y de inversión

title.original: IBM shares plunge 25% as customers shift spending to AI
title.en: IBM shares plunge 25% as customers shift spending to AI
title.zh: IBM股价暴跌25%，客户转向人工智能支出
title.es: Las acciones de IBM caen 25% ante el cambio del gasto de clientes hacia IA

summary.original: The Financial Times reports that shares of IBM dropped 25% as customers shifted their spending towards artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. IBM's Chief Executive, Arvind Krishna, acknowledged that the company 'faltered' while clients rapidly invested in servers and storage to support AI deployment. This share price plunge reflects market concerns about IBM's ability to keep pace with changing demands in AI and tech spending. This summary directly reflects the information provided by the Financial Times.
summary.en: The Financial Times reports that shares of IBM dropped 25% as customers shifted their spending towards artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. IBM's Chief Executive, Arvind Krishna, acknowledged that the company 'faltered' while clients rapidly invested in servers and storage to support AI deployment. This share price plunge reflects market concerns about IBM's ability to keep pace with changing demands in AI and tech spending. This summary directly reflects the information provided by the Financial Times.
summary.zh: 《金融时报》报道，IBM股价下跌25%，因客户将支出转向人工智能技术。IBM首席执行官阿尔文·克里希纳承认公司在客户快速投资AI支持的服务器和存储时有所“失策”。股价暴跌反映市场对IBM在人工智能及技术支出领域跟进能力的担忧。以上信息来自《金融时报》。
summary.es: El Financial Times informa que las acciones de IBM cayeron un 25% debido a que los clientes desplazaron su gasto hacia tecnologías de inteligencia artificial (IA). El director ejecutivo de IBM, Arvind Krishna, reconoció que la empresa

title.original: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
title.en: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
title.zh: 特朗普取消霍尔木兹收费威胁，美恢复对伊朗港口封锁
title.es: Trump cancela amenaza de tarifa del 20% en carga de Hormuz mientras EE.UU. reanuda bloqueo a puertos iraníes

summary.original: The BBC World article states that President Donald Trump reversed his earlier threat to impose a 20% fee on all cargo shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. This decision came as the US resumed a naval blockade of Iranian ports to counter Iran's influence over the waterway. Trump announced that instead of the fee, the US would pursue "massive" trade and investment deals with Gulf states but did not provide details. The US Central Command noted ongoing strikes against Iranian targets aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack commercial shipping. Iran responded with military actions and asserted it would remain in control of the Strait. Trump described the US as the "guardian" of the strait and stated that oil flow was continuing strongly due to US military power. The article also mentions the impacts on oil prices and shipping traffic, which have slowed to a two-month low. This summary is based on the content supplied by the BBC and is source-limited.
summary.en: The BBC World article states that President Donald Trump reversed his earlier threat to impose a 20% fee on all cargo shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. This decision came as the US resumed a naval blockade of Iranian ports to counter Iran's influence over the waterway. Trump announced that instead of the fee, the US would pursue "massive" trade and investment deals with Gulf states but did not provide details. The US Central Command noted ongoing strikes against Iranian targets aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack commercial shipping. Iran responded with military actions and asserted it would remain in control of the Strait. Trump described the US as the "guardian" of the strait and stated that oil flow was continuing strongly due to US military power. The article also mentions the impacts on oil prices and shipping traffic, which have slowed to a two-month low. This summary is based on the content supplied by the BBC and is source-limited.
summary.zh: BBC世界报道，总统唐纳德·特朗普撤销了早前对霍尔木兹海峡货物征收20%费用的威胁。此举发生在美国重新封锁伊朗港口以对抗伊朗影响该水道之际。特朗普宣布将以“庞大”的贸易和投资协议取代收费，但未公布详情。美国中央司令部称持续打击伊朗目标以削弱其攻击商业航运能力。伊朗则采取军事行动并声称将掌控海峡。特朗普称美国为该海峡“守护者”，并称因美军事力量油流保持畅通。文章还提及油价和航运流量降至两个月低点。以上内容基于BBC报道。
summary.es: El artículo de BBC Mundo indica que el presidente Donald Trump revirtió su amenaza de imponer una tarifa del 20% a toda carga que atraviese el Estrecho de Hormuz. Esta decisión ocurre mientras EE.UU. retomó un bloqueo naval a puertos iraníes para contrarrestar la influencia de Irán en la vía. Trump anunció que en lugar de la tarifa, EE.UU. buscará acuerdos comerciales y de inversión

title.original: How do young people feel about AI? 7 teens weigh in
title.en: How do young people feel about AI? 7 teens weigh in
title.zh: 年轻人如何看待人工智能？7位青少年表达观点
title.es: ¿Qué piensan los jóvenes sobre la IA? Siete adolescentes opinan

summary.original: An NPR News segment explores how young people feel about artificial intelligence by featuring opinions from seven teenagers across the United States. The content aims to provide perspectives on growing up and learning in the age of AI. The evidence is limited to the described program and a brief player description without detailed commentary from the teens themselves. Therefore, this summary is source-limited.
summary.en: An NPR News segment explores how young people feel about artificial intelligence by featuring opinions from seven teenagers across the United States. The content aims to provide perspectives on growing up and learning in the age of AI. The evidence is limited to the described program and a brief player description without detailed commentary from the teens themselves. Therefore, this summary is source-limited.
summary.zh: NPR新闻节目采访了来自美国的七位青少年，探讨他们对人工智能的看法。内容旨在展示年轻人在人工智能时代成长和学习的观点。报道仅限于节目描述及简短介绍，缺乏青少年详细评论。以上总结依据NPR内容。
summary.es: Un segmento de NPR News explora cómo los jóvenes sienten sobre la inteligencia artificial presentando opiniones de siete adolescentes de Estados Unidos. El contenido pretende ofrecer perspectivas sobre crecer y aprender en la era de la IA. La evidencia se limita al programa descrito y una breve descripción, sin comentarios detallados de los adolescentes. Por ello, este resumen está limitado a la fuente.

title.original: Marco Rubio’s Disappearing Signal Chat
title.en: Marco Rubio’s Disappearing Signal Chat
title.zh: 马可·鲁比奥的消失信号聊天
title.es: La conversación desaparecida de Marco Rubio en Signal

summary.original: The Atlantic reports that Secretary of State Marco Rubio is legally required to retain records created as part of his government job, yet his use of disappearing message functions on the Signal app has been under scrutiny. Initially, the State Department told a court last year that Rubio's phone software, LeapXpert, automatically preserved all Signal messages, even if configured to auto-delete, and that Rubio did not use disappearing messages. However, by spring 2026, that assertion was dropped, as evidence emerged that Rubio had enabled disappearing messages in a Signal chat related to administration matters. The State Department maintains that Signal remains an approved communication tool and that LeapXpert supports compliance with record-keeping, even when disappearing messages are used. The Foreign Affairs Manual prohibits ephemeral messaging features for record integrity, but the State Department has provided additional guidance recognizing the LeapXpert software's archiving capabilities. Advocacy group Democracy Forward is litigating to obtain Signal chats and is evaluating further actions based on newly revealed communications involving Rubio and other officials. The State Department has not clarified why disappearing messages were activated if archiving software was in place.
summary.en: The Atlantic reports that Secretary of State Marco Rubio is legally required to retain records created as part of his government job, yet his use of disappearing message functions on the Signal app has been under scrutiny. Initially, the State Department told a court last year that Rubio's phone software, LeapXpert, automatically preserved all Signal messages, even if configured to auto-delete, and that Rubio did not use disappearing messages. However, by spring 2026, that assertion was dropped, as evidence emerged that Rubio had enabled disappearing messages in a Signal chat related to administration matters. The State Department maintains that Signal remains an approved communication tool and that LeapXpert supports compliance with record-keeping, even when disappearing messages are used. The Foreign Affairs Manual prohibits ephemeral messaging features for record integrity, but the State Department has provided additional guidance recognizing the LeapXpert software's archiving capabilities. Advocacy group Democracy Forward is litigating to obtain Signal chats and is evaluating further actions based on newly revealed communications involving Rubio and other officials. The State Department has not clarified why disappearing messages were activated if archiving software was in place.
summary.zh: 《大西洋月刊》报道国务卿马可·鲁比奥依法需保存政府工作相关记录，但其使用Signal应用的消失消息功能受到审查。最初，国务院去年对法院称其使用的LeapXpert软件会自动保存所有Signal消息，即使设置为自动删除，且鲁比奥未使用消失消息。但到2026年春，该说法被放弃，出现证据显示鲁比奥启用了涉及政府事务的消失消息。国务院称Signal仍是批准的通信工具，LeapXpert支持记录保存。外交事务手册禁止使用短暂消息以保持记录完整，但国务院提供额外指导认可LeapXpert的归档能力。倡导团体“民主前进”正诉讼获取Signal聊天记录并评估针对新披露的通信采取行动。国务院未说明为何启用消失消息。
summary.es: The Atlantic informa que el Secretario de Estado Marco Rubio está legalmente obligado a conservar registros de su trabajo gubernamental, pero su uso de mensajes desaparecidos en la app Signal está bajo escrutinio. Inicialmente, el Departamento de Estado aseguró que el software LeapXpert guardaba automáticamente todos los mensajes incluso si estaban configurados para eliminarse, y que Rubio no usaba mensajes desaparecidos. Sin embargo, en primavera de 2026, esa afirmación fue retirada al surgir pruebas de que Rubio usó mensajes que desaparecen en chats relacionados con asuntos administrativos. El Departamento mantiene que Signal sigue siendo una herramienta aprobada y que LeapXpert permite cumplir con la conservación de registros. El Manual de Asuntos Exteriores prohíbe mensajes efímeros para integridad de registros, pero se reconocen las capacidades de archivo de LeapXpert. La organización Democracy Forward litiga para obtener los chats y evalúa acciones basadas en nuevas comunicaciones reveladas. El Departamento no ha aclarado por qué se activaron mensajes que desaparecen.

title.original: The Bizarre Partnership Between the U.S. and Venezuela
title.en: The Bizarre Partnership Between the U.S. and Venezuela
title.zh: 美国与委内瑞拉的奇异合作伙伴关系
title.es: La extraña alianza entre EE.UU. y Venezuela

summary.original: The Atlantic describes the recent, unusual humanitarian partnership between the U.S. and Venezuela following deadly earthquakes on June 24, 2026, which killed at least 4,490 people and left nearly 18,000 homeless. After years of hostility and severed diplomatic ties since 2019, American diplomats reopened an embassy building in March 2026. U.S. Marines are assisting as air traffic controllers and in port operations, while State Department teams distribute aid. The relationship contrasts with prior U.S. policy aimed at opposition and democratic transition, as the U.S. government now works with Venezuela's interim government, led by Delcy Rodríguez. Meetings between U.S. officials and Venezuelan leaders linked to repression have caused controversy. The earthquake has complicated the timeline for democratic elections outlined in U.S. policy. Venezuelan officials have defended their disaster response amid criticism. The State Department emphasizes focusing on humanitarian aid and avoiding political issues at this time, though Venezuela's most popular opposition figure was reportedly barred from returning after the earthquake. The briefing is based on available information from the article and is source-limited.
summary.en: The Atlantic describes the recent, unusual humanitarian partnership between the U.S. and Venezuela following deadly earthquakes on June 24, 2026, which killed at least 4,490 people and left nearly 18,000 homeless. After years of hostility and severed diplomatic ties since 2019, American diplomats reopened an embassy building in March 2026. U.S. Marines are assisting as air traffic controllers and in port operations, while State Department teams distribute aid. The relationship contrasts with prior U.S. policy aimed at opposition and democratic transition, as the U.S. government now works with Venezuela's interim government, led by Delcy Rodríguez. Meetings between U.S. officials and Venezuelan leaders linked to repression have caused controversy. The earthquake has complicated the timeline for democratic elections outlined in U.S. policy. Venezuelan officials have defended their disaster response amid criticism. The State Department emphasizes focusing on humanitarian aid and avoiding political issues at this time, though Venezuela's most popular opposition figure was reportedly barred from returning after the earthquake. The briefing is based on available information from the article and is source-limited.
summary.zh: 《大西洋月刊》描述了2026年6月24日地震后美国与委内瑞拉之间罕见的人道主义合作，地震造成至少4490人死亡，近18000人无家可归。2019年以来两国关系紧张、断交后，2026年3月美方重新开放使馆。美国海军陆战队协助空管和港口作业，国务院团队分发援助。此举与以往支持反对派和民主转型的政策不同，美国现与由德尔西·罗德里格斯领导的委内瑞拉临时政府合作。美国官员与受压制领导人接触引发争议。地震使美国政策中的民主选举时间表复杂化。委内瑞拉官员为应对灾难辩护。国务院强调以人道援助为主，避免政治，尽管最受欢迎的反对派人物被拒入境。报道基于文章可得信息，信息有限。
summary.es: The Atlantic describe la inusual colaboración humanitaria reciente entre EE.UU. y Venezuela tras un terremoto mortal el 24 de junio de 2026, que causó al menos 4,490 muertes y dejó casi 18,000 sin hogar. Tras años de hostilidad y rompimiento diplomático desde 2019, diplomáticos estadounidenses reabrieron una embajada en marzo de 2026. Marines ayudan en control de tráfico aéreo y operación portuaria, y equipos del Departamento de Estado distribuyen ayuda. Esta relación difiere de políticas previas que apoyaban la oposición y transición democrática; EE.UU. trabaja ahora con el gobierno interino de Delcy Rodríguez. Encuentros con líderes vinculados a la represión generaron controversia. El terremoto complicó el calendario electoral previsto. Oficiales venezolanos defienden su respuesta. El Departamento de Estado enfoca en la ayuda humanitaria evitando asuntos políticos, aunque la figura opositora más popular fue impedida de regresar. Resumen limitado a la información disponible.

title.original: In the aftermath of deadly shootings, ICE pauses most traffic stops
title.en: In the aftermath of deadly shootings, ICE pauses most traffic stops
title.zh: 致命枪击后，ICE暂停大部分交通拦截
title.es: Después de tiroteos mortales, ICE pausa la mayoría de las paradas de vehículos

summary.original: NPR News reports that U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) will pause most non-urgent vehicle stops following two deadly shootings in under a week, according to Maine Senator Angus King's office. The report does not provide further details on the circumstances of the shootings or the timeline for resuming such stops. This summary is source-limited due to brief available information.
summary.en: NPR News reports that U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) will pause most non-urgent vehicle stops following two deadly shootings in under a week, according to Maine Senator Angus King's office. The report does not provide further details on the circumstances of the shootings or the timeline for resuming such stops. This summary is source-limited due to brief available information.
summary.zh: NPR新闻报道，美国移民与海关执法局（ICE）将在短短一周内发生两起致命枪击事件后暂停大部分非紧急车辆拦截，来自缅因州参议员Angus King办公室的信息。报道未提供更多关于枪击详情或恢复拦截时间的信息，本摘要信息有限。
summary.es: NPR informa que el Servicio de Inmigración y Control de Aduanas (ICE) de EE.UU. pausará la mayoría de las paradas de vehículos no urgentes tras dos tiroteos fatales en menos de una semana, según la oficina del senador Angus King de Maine. El reporte no ofrece detalles adicionales sobre los tiroteos ni sobre cuándo reanudarán las paradas. Este resumen está limitado por la escasa información disponible.

title.original: Senate Democrats Block Defense Bill Over War in Iran
title.en: Senate Democrats Block Defense Bill Over War in Iran
title.zh: 参议院民主党因伊朗战争阻碍国防法案
title.es: Senadores demócratas bloquean proyecto de ley de defensa por guerra en Irán

summary.original: The New York Times reports that Senate Democrats blocked a defense bill amid ongoing political debate over the Middle East conflict, specifically the war in Iran. The vote reflects how bipartisan support for an otherwise typically popular defense measure has been diminished due to differing views on the conflict. Further detailed information about the bill or the voting process is not included in the provided text; this summary is source-limited.
summary.en: The New York Times reports that Senate Democrats blocked a defense bill amid ongoing political debate over the Middle East conflict, specifically the war in Iran. The vote reflects how bipartisan support for an otherwise typically popular defense measure has been diminished due to differing views on the conflict. Further detailed information about the bill or the voting process is not included in the provided text; this summary is source-limited.
summary.zh: 《纽约时报》报道，参议院民主党因中东冲突特别是伊朗战争的持续政治争议，阻止了国防法案的通过。该表决反映了尽管该法案通常受欢迎，但在冲突观点分歧下，跨党派支持减弱。有关法案细节与投票过程未在文本中详细说明，摘要基于有限信息。
summary.es: The New York Times informa que senadores demócratas bloquearon un proyecto de ley de defensa debido a debates políticos sobre el conflicto en Medio Oriente, específicamente la guerra en Irán. title.original: 'If we die, we die together': Wife of man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane speaks of ordeal
title.en: 'If we die, we die together': Wife of man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane speaks of ordeal
title.zh: “如果我们死了，就一起死”：差点被吸出莱恩航空飞机的男子妻子讲述经历
title.es: “Si morimos, morimos juntos”: esposa del hombre casi succionado de un avión de Ryanair relata su experiencia

summary.original: Svetlana Grković described an incident on a Ryanair flight from Thessaloniki to Memmingen in which her husband, Ljubisa Karović, was nearly sucked head-first out of a cabin window after it shattered. She said his "right shoulder and head were outside the plane" and that two other passengers helped pull him back inside. Grković told Serbian media that her husband is seriously injured, in shock, and unable to communicate or fully remember the event. The plane experienced rapid decompression about ten minutes into the flight, dropping 9,000 feet before returning safely to Thessaloniki. The aircraft was operated by Ryanair’s Malta Air subsidiary, and the incident is under investigation by Greek and international aviation authorities. Passengers reported hearing an explosion and feeling extreme decompression. This summary is source-limited to the details in the BBC report.
summary.en: Svetlana Grković described an incident on a Ryanair flight from Thessaloniki to Memmingen in which her husband, Ljubisa Karović, was nearly sucked head-first out of a cabin window after it shattered. She said his "right shoulder and head were outside the plane" and that two other passengers helped pull him back inside. Grković told Serbian media that her husband is seriously injured, in shock, and unable to communicate or fully remember the event. The plane experienced rapid decompression about ten minutes into the flight, dropping 9,000 feet before returning safely to Thessaloniki. The aircraft was operated by Ryanair’s Malta Air subsidiary, and the incident is under investigation by Greek and international aviation authorities. Passengers reported hearing an explosion and feeling extreme decompression. This summary is source-limited to the details in the BBC report.
summary.zh: 斯韦特拉娜·格尔科维奇描述了她丈夫柳比沙·卡罗维奇在从塞萨洛尼基飞往梅明根的莱恩航空航班上，因机舱窗户破裂差点被吸出机外的事件。她说丈夫的“右肩和头部都在机外”，有两名乘客协助将其拉回机舱。格尔科维奇向塞尔维亚媒体称丈夫受重伤、处于休克状态，无法沟通或完全记得发生的事。航班起飞约十分钟后快速减压，下降9000英尺后安全返回塞萨洛尼基。该飞机由莱恩航空旗下的马耳他航空运营，事件正由希腊及国际航空当局调查。乘客报告听到爆炸声并感受强烈减压。摘要基于BBC报道内容。
summary.es: Svetlana Grković relató un incidente en un vuelo de Ryanair desde Tesalónica a Memmingen donde su esposo, Ljubisa Karović, casi fue succionado por una ventana de cabina rota. Dijo que su "hombro derecho y cabeza estaban fuera del avión" y que dos pasajeros lo ayudaron a volver adentro. Grković contó a medios serbios que su esposo está gravemente herido, en shock y no puede comunicarse ni recordar bien el evento. El avión sufrió una descompresión rápida diez minutos después del vuelo, bajando 9,000 pies antes de regresar seguro a Tesalónica. La aeronave pertenece a Malta Air, subsidiaria de Ryanair, y el incidente está bajo investigación griega e internacional. Pasajeros oyeron una explosión y sintieron fuerte descompresión. Resumen basado en el informe de la BBC.

title.original: Strait of Hormuz 'faultline' exposes weakness of the US-Iran deal
title.en: Strait of Hormuz 'faultline' exposes weakness of the US-Iran deal
title.zh: 霍尔木兹海峡“断层线”暴露美伊协议弱点
title.es: La 'falla' del Estrecho de Ormuz expone la debilidad del acuerdo EE.UU.-Irán

summary.original: The BBC article discusses the fragile situation following the US-Iran tentative ceasefire deal, focusing on the Strait of Hormuz as a critical point of contention. Iran insists on control over this strategic maritime corridor, which neither military, economic, nor diplomatic pressures from the US can overturn. The memorandum of understanding from June includes a 14-point plan with differing interpretations between the US and Iran regarding point five about the safe passage of commercial vessels. Iran views it as a green light for its control, while the US sees it as requiring free global passage. Recent attacks on vessels near Oman's coastline were attributed to a 'rogue unit' within Iran's Revolutionary Guard, underscoring tensions. Iran's parliament introduced a new bill to manage the strait, emphasizing its inalienable right to control. This stance has caused friction with neighboring countries like Qatar, Oman, and the UAE, especially over Iran's intention to impose "service fees". Mediation continues, but the deal's vague terms and deep mistrust contribute to ongoing instability and economic difficulties in Iran, including high inflation and job losses. This summary is based on the information provided by the BBC article and is source-limited.
summary.en: The BBC article discusses the fragile situation following the US-Iran tentative ceasefire deal, focusing on the Strait of Hormuz as a critical point of contention. Iran insists on control over this strategic maritime corridor, which neither military, economic, nor diplomatic pressures from the US can overturn. Iran views it as a green light for its control, while the US sees it as requiring free global passage. Recent attacks on vessels near Oman's coastline were attributed to a 'rogue unit' within Iran's Revolutionary Guard, underscoring tensions. Iran's parliament introduced a new bill to manage the strait, emphasizing its inalienable right to control. This stance has caused friction with neighboring countries like Qatar, Oman, and the UAE, especially over Iran's intention to impose "service fees". Mediation continues, but the deal's vague terms and deep mistrust contribute to ongoing instability and economic difficulties in Iran, including high inflation and job losses. This summary is based on the information provided by the BBC article and is source-limited.

summary.es: El artículo de la BBC destaca la situación frágil tras el acuerdo de alto el fuego tentativo EE.UU.-Irán, con el Estrecho de Ormuz como foco de disputa, interpretaciones divergentes del paso seguro, tensiones regionales y dificultades económicas en Irán.

title.original: E. Jean Carroll receives $5.6 million from Trump in sexual abuse and defamation case
title.en: E. Jean Carroll receives $5.6 million from Trump in sexual abuse and defamation case
title.zh: E. Jean Carroll在性侵和诽谤案中获特朗普赔付560万美元
title.es: E. Jean Carroll recibe 5.6 millones de dólares de Trump en caso de abuso sexual y difamación

summary.original: E. Jean Carroll has received approximately $5.625 million in damages from President Trump, following a 2023 federal jury finding him liable for sexually abusing and defaming her. The payment was delayed due to appeals filed by Trump. Carroll's attorney stated that the damages payment corresponds to the unanimous verdict from three years ago. The final sum includes three years' worth of interest. The Supreme Court declined to hear Trump's appeal, prompting U.S. District Judge Lewis A. Kaplan to order the payment. Carroll had accused Trump of sexual assault in a 2019 memoir, an allegation he denied and responded to with defamation. A separate defamation suit filed by Carroll involving an $83 million award remains under appeal, with courts upholding the award so far. This summary is based solely on the provided NPR text.
summary.en: E. Jean Carroll has received approximately $5.625 million in damages from President Trump, following a 2023 federal jury finding him liable for sexually abusing and defaming her. The payment was delayed due to appeals filed by Trump. Carroll's attorney stated that the damages payment corresponds to the unanimous verdict from three years ago. The final sum includes three years' worth of interest. The Supreme Court declined to hear Trump's appeal, prompting U.S. District Judge Lewis A. Kaplan to order the payment. Carroll had accused Trump of sexual assault in a 2019 memoir, an allegation he denied and responded to with defamation. A separate defamation suit filed by Carroll involving an $83 million award remains under appeal, with courts upholding the award so far. This summary is based solely on the provided NPR text.
summary.zh: 根据2023年联邦陪审团裁定特朗普对E. Jean Carroll性侵和诽谤负责，总统特朗普赔付其约562.5万美元的赔偿金，因特朗普提出上诉导致付款延迟。赔偿包括三年利息，最高法院拒绝受理特朗普的上诉后，法官下令付款。Carroll于2019年回忆录中指控特朗普性侵，特朗普否认并反诉诽谤。另一起涉及8300万美元赔偿的诽谤案尚在上诉中。该摘要仅基于NPR提供文本。
summary.es: E. Jean Carroll recibió aproximadamente 5.625 millones de dólares en daños de parte del presidente Trump, tras un fallo unánime en 2023 que lo declaró responsable de abuso sexual y difamación. El pago fue retrasado por apelaciones de Trump. La suma incluye tres años de intereses. La Corte Suprema rechazó la apelación de Trump, lo que llevó a un juez federal a ordenar el pago. Carroll acusó a Trump en su libro de 2019; él negó y respondió con una demanda por difamación. Otro juicio por difamación con una indemnización de 83 millones está en apelación. Resumen basado solo en el texto de NPR.

title.original: The Shooter in Maine Was a New ICE Recruit
title.en: The Shooter in Maine Was a New ICE Recruit
title.zh: 缅因州枪手为新入职ICE官员
title.es: El tirador en Maine era un recluta nuevo de ICE

summary.original: An ICE official who fatally shot a 26-year-old Colombian man during a vehicle stop in Maine was a new recruit who had joined ICE this year from the Department of Veterans Affairs Police and had been in federal law enforcement since 2017. Following this second fatal shooting in a week, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin ordered an immediate suspension of vehicle stops nationwide for ICE officers except when cooperating with other police agencies. The suspension was communicated by top ICE official Liana Castano. The agency has increased immigration arrests to about 2,000 daily, with many occurring during stops by officers in unmarked cars. Concerns have been raised about insufficient training for vehicle stops and lack of clear policies on the use of force during such stops, especially amid a rapid expansion and fast-tracking of training for new ICE officers. No body camera footage is available of the incidents, and internal investigations are ongoing. This briefing is source-limited to information from The Atlantic text.
summary.en: An ICE official who fatally shot a 26-year-old Colombian man during a vehicle stop in Maine was a new recruit who had joined ICE this year from the Department of Veterans Affairs Police and had been in federal law enforcement since 2017. Following this second fatal shooting in a week, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin ordered an immediate suspension of vehicle stops nationwide for ICE officers except when cooperating with other police agencies. The suspension was communicated by top ICE official Liana Castano. The agency has increased immigration arrests to about 2,000 daily, with many occurring during stops by officers in unmarked cars. Concerns have been raised about insufficient training for vehicle stops and lack of clear policies on the use of force during such stops, especially amid a rapid expansion and fast-tracking of training for new ICE officers. No body camera footage is available of the incidents, and internal investigations are ongoing. This briefing is source-limited to information from The Atlantic text.
summary.zh: 缅因州一名ICE官员在拦车行动中射杀一名26岁哥伦比亚男子，该官员今年从退伍军人事务警察部门调入ICE，自2017年起从事联邦执法工作。该事件是该周第二起致命枪击，国土安全部长即刻暂停全国ICE车辆拦检，除非配合其他执法机构。ICE逮捕行动增加至每天约2000起，多为无标记车辆执行。因快速扩招和缩短新官员培训，拦截时使用武力的政策和培训受质疑。无执法记录仪录像，内部调查进行中。该摘要仅基于The Atlantic文本。
summary.es: Un oficial de ICE que disparó fatalmente a un hombre colombiano de 26 años en Maine era un recluta nuevo que se unió este año desde la Policía de Asuntos de Veteranos y trabaja en la aplicación federal desde 2017. Tras este segundo tiroteo fatal en una semana, el secretario de Seguridad Nacional suspendió de inmediato las paradas de vehículos de ICE en todo el país, salvo en cooperación con otras agencias policiales. ICE ha incrementado arrestos migratorios a cerca de 2,000 diarios, muchos durante paradas en vehículos sin distintivos. Hay preocupaciones sobre la falta de entrenamiento y políticas claras en uso de fuerza durante estas paradas, especialmente por la rápida expansión y formación acelerada de nuevos agentes. No hay grabaciones de cámaras corporales y se realizan investigaciones internas. Resumen basado en texto de The Atlantic.
